plan: Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETFs. Trim CRWD to 10% and SMH to 8% to reduce single-name and sector concentration. Increase QQQI to 25% and SPY to 35% for income and broad market exposure. Watch ARM for a potential 5% allocation as a high R/R quality tech name.
conservative.read: This mode targets capital preservation with a realistic ~1.5%/month outlook. It significantly reduces risk by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, but also limits potential upside. Expect lower volatility and smaller drawdowns.

plan: Trim SOXL to 3% and SPAL to 1% to reduce leveraged ETF exposure. Trim CRWD to 10% and SMH to 10% to reduce concentration. Increase QQQI to 20% for a slight income boost. Maintain SPY at 32% for broad market exposure. Buy PANW for a 5% allocation as a high R/R quality tech name.
balanced.read: This mode aims for risk-first growth with a realistic ~2.5%/month outlook. It balances growth with moderate risk reduction by trimming leverage and concentration. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns compared to conservative or aggressive modes.

plan: Increase SOXL to 8% and SPAL to 3% to lean into leveraged ETFs. Add to CRWD to 15% and SMH to 15% for increased concentration in high-growth sectors. Buy ARM for a 7% allocation and PANW for 6% as high R/R scanner setups. Trim SPY to 25% to free up capital for concentrated bets.
aggressive.read: This mode targets aggressive growth with a realistic ~4%/month outlook, but comes with significantly higher risk. Leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) decay daily and can lead to substantial drawdowns, especially with high concentration. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage the increased volatility and potential for significant losses.

plan: Sell all SOXL, SPAL, and CRWD to eliminate leveraged ETFs and high-beta single names. Trim SMH to 5% to further reduce high-beta exposure. Maximize QQQI to 40% and increase SPY to 40% for income and broad index stability. Trim QQQ to 10% to reduce growth-oriented index exposure.
income.read: This mode prioritizes steady monthly distributions with a realistic ~1%/month outlook, focusing on parking cash for yield. While aiming for low drawdown, distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff. This is income-focused, not a savings account, and still carries market risk.
